title: Holiday and event promotional lightboxes with seasonal campaigns
description:
dispatched_by: mayor

Create a reusable promotional lightbox system for holidays, sales events, and seasonal campaigns. These should be eye-catching, on-brand, and drive engagement with featured products.

1. PROMOTIONAL LIGHTBOX ENGINE (masterPage.js + new backend module)
   
   Backend: src/backend/promotions.web.js
   - getActivePromotion() — queries a Promotions CMS collection for currently active campaign
   - Promotion record fields: title, subtitle, backgroundImage, products (array of product IDs), startDate, endDate, discountCode, discountPercent, theme (holiday/sale/event/seasonal)
   - Returns null if no active promotion, so lightbox stays dormant
   
   masterPage.js integration:
   - On page load, call getActivePromotion()
   - If active promo exists AND user hasn't dismissed it this session → show lightbox
   - Lightbox auto-shows 3 seconds after page load (not instant — let page render first)
   - Store dismissal in sessionStorage per promotion ID

2. LIGHTBOX TEMPLATES BY THEME
   
   All use the Carolina Futons design tokens but with seasonal flair:
   
   HOLIDAY themes:
   - Christmas/Winter: Mountain scene with snow, evergreen accents, cozy cabin vibes
     Colors: Deep espresso bg, white snow accents, coral gift-ribbon highlights
   - Fourth of July: Red/white/blue mountain sunset, patriotic savings
   - Labor Day / Memorial Day: Classic sale themes — biggest furniture sale weekends
   - Valentine's Day: Cozy couple vibes, bedroom furniture focus
   - Black Friday / Cyber Monday: Bold urgency, countdown timer, doorbusters
   
   SEASONAL themes:
   - Spring Refresh: Light sand + sky blue, fresh starts, new collections
   - Summer Living: Outdoor/patio focus, bright coral + mountain blue
   - Fall Cozy: Warm espresso + mauve, layering textures, throw pillows
   - Back to School / College: Dorm-size futons, compact solutions
   
   EVENT themes:
   - Flash Sale: Countdown timer, single-day urgency, pulsing CTA
   - New Arrival: Spotlight single product with multiple images
   - Clearance: 'Last Chance' messaging, limited stock indicators
   - Anniversary Sale: 'Celebrating 35 Years' (1991-2026), heritage story

3. LIGHTBOX UI ELEMENTS
   - Full-screen overlay with centered modal
   - Hero image or illustration at top
   - Headline in Playfair Display (seasonal theming)
   - Subhead with offer details
   - Countdown timer for time-limited events (days:hours:mins:secs)
   - Product carousel showing 3-4 featured sale items with Quick Add buttons
   - Discount code display with copy-to-clipboard
   - Email capture: 'Get early access to our next event'
   - CTA button: 'Shop the Sale' → links to filtered category page
   - Dismiss: X button + 'Maybe later' text link
   
   Element IDs: #promoLightbox, #promoOverlay, #promoHeroImage, #promoTitle, #promoSubtitle, #promoCountdown, #promoRepeater, #promoImage, #promoName, #promoPrice, #promoOrigPrice, #promoQuickAdd, #promoCode, #promoCopyCode, #promoEmailInput, #promoEmailSubmit, #promoCTA, #promoClose, #promoDismiss

4. CMS COLLECTION: Promotions
   Fields: title (text), subtitle (text), theme (text), heroImage (image), startDate (datetime), endDate (datetime), discountCode (text), discountPercent (number), ctaUrl (text), ctaText (text), productIds (text — comma-separated), isActive (boolean)

title: Interactive fabric swatch visualizer: see swatches applied to futon product images
description:
dispatched_by: mayor

THE differentiator feature. Carolina Futons has 700+ fabric swatches — let customers SEE how different fabrics look on the actual futon before buying. This is the kind of feature that makes people choose Carolina Futons over competitors.

CONCEPT: On the Product Page, when a customer selects a fabric swatch, the product image updates to show that fabric applied to the futon. This can work through multiple approaches depending on available assets:

APPROACH 1: VARIANT IMAGE MATCHING (simplest, works with Wix Stores)
- Each product variant (fabric/finish) already has its own image set in Wix Stores
- When customer selects a swatch, the gallery switches to that variant's images
- This is the most reliable approach — uses standard Wix Stores product media
- Enhancement: Show a grid of fabric swatch thumbnails (small colored squares) below the gallery
- Clicking a swatch = selecting that variant = gallery updates to variant images
- Works with existing handleCustomVariantChange() flow in Product Page

APPROACH 2: SWATCH OVERLAY (advanced, works with any product)
- Store fabric swatch images (small square texture samples) in Wix Media Manager
- Create a FabricSwatches CMS collection: swatchId, swatchName, swatchImage (wix:image://), colorFamily, material, availableForProducts (comma-separated product IDs)
- On PDP, query available swatches for the current product
- Display swatch grid: small colored thumbnails (60x60px) in a scrollable row
- On swatch click: apply CSS/canvas overlay tinting to the product main image
- Use color extraction from the swatch to create a tint layer
- This gives an APPROXIMATION — pair with 'Request Free Swatches' for tactile confirmation

APPROACH 3: PRE-RENDERED FABRIC VIEWS (premium, best results)
- For top-selling products, create product images in each popular fabric
- Store in Media Manager with naming convention: product-slug_fabric-name.jpg
- Map in CMS: ProductFabricImages collection (productId, fabricName, imageUrl)
- Gallery dynamically loads the matched fabric image set
- Most realistic result but requires more photo assets

IMPLEMENTATION (combine Approach 1 + 2):

Product Page changes (Product Page.ve2z7.js):
1. New function initSwatchSelector():
   - Query FabricSwatches CMS for swatches available for currentProduct
   - Render swatch grid below gallery: scrollable horizontal row of 60x60 fabric thumbnails
   - Each swatch shows: color preview, fabric name tooltip on hover
   - Click swatch → call handleCustomVariantChange() if variant exists for that fabric
   - If no exact variant, apply color tint overlay to #productMainImage
   - Highlight selected swatch with mountain blue (#5B8FA8) border

2. Swatch section UI:
   - Section title: 'Choose Your Fabric' (Playfair Display)
   - Swatch count: 'Showing X of 700+ available fabrics'
   - Filter by color family: All | Neutral | Blue | Green | Red | Earth Tones
   - 'View All Swatches' link opens full swatch gallery lightbox
   - 'Request Free Swatches' button (connects to cf-8vr swatch request feature)
   
   Element IDs: #swatchSection, #swatchGrid, #swatchCount, #swatchColorFilter, #swatchViewAll, #swatchRequestLink

3. Full swatch gallery lightbox:
   - Grid of all available swatches (120x120px each)
   - Click to select + see on product
   - Search/filter by name or color family
   - Shows fabric details: material composition, care instructions
   Element IDs: #swatchGalleryModal, #swatchGalleryGrid, #swatchSearch, #swatchDetail

Backend (new or extend productRecommendations.web.js):
- getProductSwatches(productId, colorFamily?, limit?) — query FabricSwatches CMS
- getAllSwatchFamilies() — distinct color families for filter dropdown

CMS: FabricSwatches collection
- swatchId (text)
- swatchName (text) — e.g., 'Coastal Blue Tweed'
- swatchImage (image) — wix:image:// URI of 60x60 swatch photo
- colorFamily (text) — 'neutral', 'blue', 'green', 'red', 'earth'
- colorHex (text) — primary hex color for tint overlay
- material (text) — 'cotton', 'polyester', 'linen blend'
- careInstructions (text)
- availableForProducts (text) — comma-separated product IDs, or 'all'
- sortOrder (number)

Category Page integration:
- Show 'Available in 700+ fabrics' badge on product cards for items with fabric options
- Add small swatch preview dots (3-4 color circles) on grid cards
- Element IDs: #gridSwatchPreview, #gridFabricBadge

Files: src/pages/Product Page.ve2z7.js, src/pages/Category Page.u0gn0.js, src/backend/productRecommendations.web.js or new swatchService.web.js

title: Wix CMS data architecture: custom collections for enhanced product data and engagement
description:
dispatched_by: mayor

Design and implement a robust CMS data layer that goes beyond basic Wix Stores to power all engagement features. Security is paramount — all backend queries use server-side web modules, never expose CMS directly to frontend.

CORE PRINCIPLE: Wix Stores handles products/orders/cart. Custom CMS collections handle ENGAGEMENT data that Stores doesn't track. All CMS access goes through backend web modules with proper permission gates.

1. CUSTOM CMS COLLECTIONS (beyond what's already spec'd)

   Already spec'd (from memory.md): ProductAnalytics, ContactSubmissions, Wishlist, Fulfillments

   NEW collections needed:

   a) ProductBundles
      - bundleId (text, primary)
      - primaryProductId (text) — the product being viewed
      - bundledProductIds (text, comma-separated) — suggested bundle items
      - discountPercent (number) — bundle discount (default 5)
      - bundleName (text) — e.g., 'Complete Your Futon Set'
      - isActive (boolean)
      Purpose: Powers 'Frequently Bought Together' on PDP

   b) Promotions
      - title, subtitle, theme, heroImage, startDate, endDate
      - discountCode, discountPercent, ctaUrl, ctaText
      - productIds (text), isActive (boolean)
      Purpose: Powers holiday/event lightboxes

   c) CustomerEngagement
      - memberId (text)
      - eventType (text) — 'page_view', 'add_to_cart', 'wishlist_add', 'quiz_complete', 'swatch_request'
      - productId (text, optional)
      - metadata (text, JSON string) — flexible event data
      - timestamp (datetime)
      - sessionId (text)
      Purpose: Unified engagement tracking for personalization

   d) ReviewRequests
      - orderId (text)
      - customerEmail (text)
      - productIds (text)
      - scheduledDate (datetime)
      - status (text) — 'pending', 'sent', 'completed'
      - rating (number, optional)
      - reviewText (text, optional)
      Purpose: Review request scheduling and collection

   e) ReferralCodes
      - code (text, unique)
      - memberId (text) — referrer
      - discountPercent (number)
      - usedBy (text, optional) — referee memberId
      - usedAt (datetime, optional)
      - creditAmount (number) — store credit earned
      Purpose: Referral program tracking

   f) Videos
      - title (text)
      - videoUrl (text)
      - thumbnail (image)
      - productId (text, optional)
      - category (text) — futon/murphy/platform/general
      - duration (text)
      - viewCount (number)
      - isFeatured (boolean)
      Purpose: Video content management for embedded players

2. BACKEND DATA ACCESS LAYER
   Create src/backend/dataService.web.js — centralized CMS access module:
   - All queries go through server-side webMethod with Permissions.Anyone (read) or Permissions.SiteMember (write)
   - Input validation on all write operations
   - Rate limiting awareness (Wix Data API has limits)
   - Sanitize all user inputs before CMS writes (prevent injection)
   - Error handling with graceful fallbacks

3. SECURITY CONSIDERATIONS
   - NEVER query CMS directly from frontend page code
   - All writes require authentication check (wix-users-backend.currentUser)
   - Sanitize all string inputs (strip HTML, limit length)
   - Use Wix Permissions.SiteMember for member-specific data
   - PII (email, phone) only in ContactSubmissions with restricted read access
   - No secrets or credentials in CMS — use Wix Secrets Manager only

Files: New src/backend/dataService.web.js, updates to existing backend modules

title: Interactive size guide and room planner
description:
Current size information is just a dropdown (Full, Queen, Twin) with dimensions in a text accordion. For furniture purchases, customers need visual sizing tools to reduce returns and increase confidence.

CURRENT STATE:
- ProductOptions.js: size dropdown with stock check
- ProductDetails.js: dimensions in accordion text
- Shipping Policy.js: mentions door/hallway clearance
- No visual size tools

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Visual Size Guide Modal
   - Triggered from product page 'Size Guide' link (add next to size dropdown)
   - SVG/illustration showing product dimensions (W x D x H) with labels
   - Side-by-side comparison of Full vs Queen vs Twin with human silhouette for scale
   - Mattress thickness comparison
   - Folded vs unfolded dimensions (critical for futons!)

2. Room Planner Calculator
   - 'Will It Fit?' section on product page
   - Input: room dimensions (length x width)
   - Input: doorway width, hallway width, stairway width
   - Output: visual room layout showing futon placement options
   - Output: clearance check — will it fit through your door? (futons are bulky)
   - Delivery access check: ground floor vs upstairs, elevator vs stairs

3. Dimension Data
   - sizeGuideData.js (public): structured dimension data per product category
   - Categories: futon frames, futon mattresses, covers, pillows
   - Fields: width, depth, height, foldedDepth, weight, boxDimensions, doorClearanceMin

4. Mobile Experience
   - Swipeable size comparison cards
   - Simple room width input (skip complex planner on mobile)
   - Clear 'fits / might not fit / won't fit' verdict

TESTING:
- Size calculation tests (clearance logic)
- Room planner layout tests
- Data validation for dimension entries

Ref: ProductOptions.js (size dropdown), ProductDetails.js (dimensions accordion), Shipping Policy.js (delivery info)

Propose visual approach. Flag: futon dimensions change dramatically between sofa and bed position — must show BOTH configurations.

- performanceMonitor.test.js: metric capture, reporting

title: Convert all image references to Wix Media Manager wix:image:// format
description:
dispatched_by: mayor

Update placeholderImages.js and all page scripts to use Wix Media Manager URL format so images auto-populate when deployed to Wix.

Wix Media Manager format: wix:image://v1/<fileId>/<filename>#originWidth=<w>&originHeight=<h>

Current state: placeholderImages.js uses Unsplash URLs. Product images from Wix Stores (product.mainMedia) are already correct — they use the Wix format natively.

What needs updating:
1. placeholderImages.js — Replace all Unsplash URLs with wix:image:// placeholder format. Use descriptive constant names (HERO_FUTON_FRAMES etc.) that map to Media Manager paths. Include comments showing the expected file name in Media Manager for each slot.

2. All hero/background images in page scripts — Use getCategoryHeroImage() which routes through placeholderImages.js

3. galleryConfig.js — Ensure image sizing uses Wix Media API transform parameters (w_400,h_400,al_c,q_80) rather than URL query params

4. Add a MEDIA_MANIFEST.md documenting every image slot:
   - Slot name, expected dimensions, where it appears on site
   - Current: Unsplash placeholder
   - Target: Media Manager path (to be filled when photos are uploaded)
   - This becomes the checklist for populating the Media Manager

Wix Media API image transforms:
- Resize: /v1/fill/w_<width>,h_<height>,al_c,q_<quality>/<filename>
- Crop: /v1/crop/w_<width>,h_<height>,x_<x>,y_<y>/<filename>
- Scale: /v1/fit/w_<width>,h_<height>/<filename>

Files: src/public/placeholderImages.js, MEDIA_MANIFEST.md (new)
